title: Rosa × klasterskii
taxonomy: Rosa × klasterskii is a hybrid rose species. It is a cross between Rosa gallica and Rosa moschata. Rosa × klasterskii is a fragrant, double-flowered rose with a pink color. The classification of Rosa × klasterskii as a rose species is recognized by the Royal Horticultural Society. Rosa × klasterskii is an old garden rose that originated in the 18th century.
uses: Rosa × klasterskii is a hybrid rose species, but specific information about its medicinal uses is not readily available in my knowledge.
benefits: Rosa × klasterskii is a type of hybrid rose that has been found to have several potential health benefits due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. Overall, Rosa × klasterskii is a valuable plant that can be used to create herbal remedies with several health benefits.
properties: Rosa × klasterskii, a hybrid rose species, is reportedly used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. The plant is said to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. parts: Rosa × klasterskii, a cross between a Rosa × centifolia and a Rosa × rubiginosa, is a rose hybrid used in perfumery and medicine.
